title: 10 best herbal creams for dandruff

remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help kill fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, reducing symptoms of dandruff such as itchiness, redness, and flaking.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness associated with dandruff, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing an irritated scalp.
Antioxidant properties: Tea tree oil contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with dandruff because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compound, cineole, in tea tree oil reduces the growth of Malassezia yeast on the scalp, which is a common contributor to dandruff. The cream's soothing and moisturizing effects also help calm irritated skin and reduce flaking, while its ability to penetrate deep into the scalp promotes healthy hair growth and scalp balance, providing long-lasting relief from dandruff symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): Cineole helps with dandruff by its antimicrobial properties, which reduce the growth of fungi and bacteria that contribute to scalp infections and irritation.
Terpinen-4-ol: Terpinen-4-ol has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation associated with dandruff.
Limonene: Limonene has antifungal properties, which help to prevent the growth of fungi that contribute to scalp infections, such as Malassezia, a common cause of dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in terpenes, particularly cineole, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat dandruff-causing microorganisms.
Barks: The barks contain compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing and treating dandruff.
Flowers: They are a rich source of terpenes, including cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which contribute to the antifungal and antibacterial properties of the creams, helping to control dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves to use in this recipe.
Combine the harvested leaves with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow for infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter and 50g of the infused melaleuca alternifolia oil in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Aloe vera gel's anti-inflammatory activity: Reduces redness, swelling, and itching associated with dandruff by suppressing inflammation and promoting soothing relief.
Aloe vera's antifungal and antibacterial properties: Combats fungal and bacterial overgrowth on the scalp that can contribute to dandruff, thereby preventing infection and promoting healthy skin.

Aloe vera's moisturizing and emollient properties: Hydrates and softens the scalp, helping to remove flakes and scales associated with dandruff, while also protecting the skin from dryness and irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with dandruff because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to calm the scalp and reduce irritation. Aloe vera creams also have antifungal properties that help to control the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common cause of dandruff. By reducing inflammation and controlling yeast growth, aloe vera creams can help to alleviate dandruff symptoms and promote a healthy scalp.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help to soothe the scalp, reduce inflammation, and prevent fungal growth that contributes to dandruff.
Acemannan: A type of polysaccharide, Acemannan has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help to calm the scalp, reduce dandruff symptoms, and promote a healthy scalp environment.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin A, Vitamin E, and Zinc): These nutrients help to regulate the skin's natural oil production, reduce cell turnover and inflammation, and promote the health of the scalp, ultimately reducing dandruff symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel: It is the most commonly used part due to its soothing and moisturizing properties, which help to calm and alleviate dandruff symptoms.
Leaves: Aloe vera juice: The juice extracted from the leaves is rich in vitamins and minerals that help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce flaking.
Leaves: Aloe vera latex: The latex, also known as the sap, contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Combine 3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the bowl and heat until melted.
Stir in 5 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil into the mixture.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for about 30 minutes.
Gently stir the cream with a spoon and transfer it to a storage container for later use.

remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: The cream's antifungal properties help to inhibit the growth of fungi that can contribute to dandruff, reducing flaking and itching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation caused by dandruff, making it more comfortable for individuals with the condition.

Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant properties help to protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dandruff development and worsening.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with dandruff because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The menthol and camphor present in these creams help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by dandruff, while the eucalyptol acts as a natural antiseptic to soothe and calm the scalp. Additionally, eucalyptus oil has been shown to reduce the growth of yeast, a common cause of dandruff, making it an effective natural remedy for managing this condition.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps with dandruff by reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of dead skin cells, thus alleviating the symptoms of dandruff.
Caffeic Acid: As a phenolic compound, caffeic acid has antifungal properties that can help control the growth of fungi and yeast on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff.

Limonene: This terpene has been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and irritation associated with dandruff, as well as promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Eucalyptus globulus are commonly used to make creams for dandruff due to their high content of eucalyptol, which has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus are used due to their ability to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin.

Essential oil from Leaves: The essential oil extracted from the leaves is used due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control the growth of microorganisms that cause dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Mix 200g of distilled water with 2g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to release the essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Add 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil to the container and melt over low heat.
Whip the mixture until it cools and thickens to a creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Lavender oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that can cause dandruff, such as Malassezia globosa, helping to reduce flaking and itching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and swelling associated with dandruff and promoting a calm scalp.

Antioxidant activity: English lavender creams contain antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dandruff and other scalp issues, and also soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with dandruff because of its soothing and antifungal properties. The calming effects of lavender oil can reduce inflammation and itching associated with dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of Malassezia yeast, a common contributor to dandruff. By balancing the scalp's natural pH and reducing fungal activity, English lavender creams can provide relief and help alleviate dandruff symptoms. Regular use can lead to a healthier, flake-free scalp.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of fungi that can contribute to dandruff.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene also has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to soothe the scalp and prevent the development of dandruff-causing fungi.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the scalp from damage, thereby preventing dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain essential oils that provide antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and calm the scalp.
Flowers: They are rich in essential oils with antimicrobial properties that help control fungal growth and reduce dandruff.
Seeds: They contain antioxidants and fatty acids that help moisturize and soothe the scalp, reducing flaking and irritation associated with dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and clean them thoroughly with a fine mesh strainer.
Combine 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, then let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and the infused lavandula angustifolia liquid in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal sesame cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dandruff, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.

Antifungal: The antifungal properties of herbal sesame cream can help combat fungal infections that contribute to dandruff, such as Malassezia furfur, by inhibiting their growth and preventing flare-ups.

Emollient and moisturizing: Herbal sesame cream's emollient and moisturizing properties help soothe and hydrate the scalp, reducing flakiness and irritation caused by dryness and itchiness associated with dandruff.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum, also known as sesame, creams helps with dandruff because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains sesamol, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy scalps. Sesamum indicum creams also contain antioxidants that help to combat free radicals, which can contribute to dandruff. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing flaking and irritation associated with dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp and hair.
remedy_constituents: Lignans: They help with dandruff by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of fungi, such as Malassezia, which contribute to the condition.

Fatty acids (oleic acid and linoleic acid): They help with dandruff by reducing inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp environment, and regulating the pH balance, which can help prevent fungal growth.

Phenolic compounds (sesamin and sesamolin): They help with dandruff by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing the symptoms of dandruff.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame seeds are commonly used to make creams for dandruff due to their rich source of antioxidants and fatty acids that soothe and moisturize the scalp.
Leaves: Sesame leaves are used in creams for dandruff because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching on the scalp.
Seeds (also): Sesame seeds are also used in creams for dandruff due to their antibacterial and antifungal properties that help control the growth of microorganisms on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50g of sesamum indicum seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 20g of the sesamum indicum powder with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 10 minutes.
Gradually add 20g of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of lavender essential oil for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Neem's antifungal properties help control the growth of yeast-like fungus, Malassezia, which is a common cause of dandruff and other scalp conditions.
Anti-inflammatory activity: Neem's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, irritation, and itching associated with dandruff, allowing the scalp to heal and recover more quickly.
Antioxidant activity: Neem's antioxidants help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and exacerbate dandruff symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica, also known as neem, creams helps with dandruff because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. Neem oil, a key component in the cream, has the ability to control the growth of yeast on the scalp, a primary cause of dandruff. The anti-inflammatory properties of neem also soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking. Additionally, neem's ability to regulate the pH balance of the scalp helps to maintain a healthy environment, preventing dandruff and promoting a clear scalp.
remedy_constituents: Limonoids: These compounds have been shown to inhibit fungal growth, which is often a contributing factor to dandruff, thereby reducing the severity of the condition.
Azadirachtin: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to soothe the scalp, reduce irritation, and prevent the growth of fungi that cause dandruff.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have been found to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control the growth of microorganisms on the scalp and reduce the symptoms of dandruff.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high concentration of neem oil, which has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties to help treat dandruff.
Leaves: Used because they contain compounds that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking associated with dandruff.
Seeds: Also used because they have been found to exhibit antiseptic properties, helping to prevent the growth of fungi that can cause dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh azadirachta indica leaves and flowers and dry them in the shade for 3 days.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Melt 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 20 minutes.
Combine the ground plant material with 50g of beeswax and 20g of vitamin e oil in a stainless steel bowl.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer for 10 minutes until it becomes a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Lemongrass cream's antifungal property helps to combat fungal infections that can cause dandruff, such as malassezia globosa, by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of lemongrass cream reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dandruff, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
Antioxidant property: Lemongrass cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin and scalp, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy scalp function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with dandruff because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial organisms that contribute to dandruff. Additionally, lemongrass has a soothing effect on the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with dandruff. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to balance the scalp's pH, promoting a healthy and flake-free scalp.
remedy_constituents: Citronellal: This sesquiterpene acts as an anti-inflammatory and antifungal agent, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent fungal growth on the scalp that can contribute to dandruff.
Geraniol: This monoterpenoid alcohol exhibits antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help control the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause dandruff and scalp irritation.

Limonene: This cyclic monoterpenoid has been shown to possess antiseptic and antifungal properties, helping to prevent the spread of fungal infections and reduce inflammation associated with dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high content of citral, a natural antifungal and antibacterial agent, which helps combat dandruff-causing fungi and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Rich in essential oils, particularly citral and geraniol, which possess antifungal and antimicrobial properties, helping to soothe and treat scalp irritations.
Stems: Containing similar essential oils as the rhyzomes, stems help to calm and balance the scalp, reducing flaking and itchiness associated with dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Steam distill the harvested cymbopogon citratus leaves at 100°c for 2 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Mix 10g of the obtained essential oil with 20g of a carrier oil such as jojoba oil in a glass container.
Add 5g of a moisturizing ingredient such as beeswax and 5g of a thickening agent such as stearic acid to the mixture.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and then heat it in a double boiler at 60°c for 10 minutes to create the herbal cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff symptoms such as redness and itching.
Antifungal property: The antifungal properties of turmeric help combat fungal overgrowth on the scalp, a common cause of dandruff, by inhibiting the growth of fungi that can exacerbate the condition.

Wound-healing and skin-soothing property: Turmeric's wound-healing and skin-soothing compounds, such as curcuminoids and polyphenols, help to calm irritated skin and promote healing of flakes and crusts associated with dandruff.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The curcumin present in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, thereby reducing the flaking and irritation associated with dandruff. Additionally, turmeric's antifungal properties help control the growth of Malassezia, a fungus that contributes to dandruff. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to balance the scalp's natural pH, promoting a healthy and dandruff-free scalp.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, thereby alleviating symptoms of dandruff.

Volatile oils (turmerones): The volatile oils present in Curcuma longa, such as turmerones, have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control the growth of fungi and bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff.

Polysaccharides (turmeric polysaccharides): Turmeric polysaccharides have been shown to have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing the severity of dandruff symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The active compound curcumin found in the rhyzomes has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and treat dandruff.
Seeds: The seeds contain a high amount of essential oils and fatty acids, which help to moisturize and calm the scalp, reducing dandruff and itchiness.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, making them effective in treating dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried curcuma longa root in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to make a tea.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of the curcuma longa tea to the beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth cream consistency within 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Herbal ginger creams contain antifungal compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help to combat fungal infections such as malassezia, a common cause of dandruff.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol that can reduce inflammation in the scalp, which helps to alleviate itching, redness, and flaking associated with dandruff.

Antioxidant property: Herbal ginger creams contain antioxidant compounds like vitamin C and E, which help to protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of flakes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The active compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the scalp, thus preventing the formation of flaky skin. Additionally, ginger's natural antifungal properties help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common cause of dandruff. This dual-action approach makes ginger creams a popular natural remedy for soothing and calming the scalp.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Helps with dandruff by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, which can contribute to flaking and itchiness.
Shogaol: Exhibits antifungal properties that can inhibit the growth of Malassezia, a yeast that can contribute to dandruff, thereby reducing flaking and itchiness.
Curcuminoid (specifically demethoxycurcumin): Possesses anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and flaking associated with dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of fungus that causes dandruff, thanks to the presence of compounds like gingerol and zingerone.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing flaking and itchiness associated with dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100g of zingiber officinale rhizomes with 200ml of carrier oil in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture at 60-70c for 2 hours in a double boiler or a warm water bath.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of candelilla wax to the mixture and melt at 70-80c.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Damask rose contains antifungal compounds like gallic acid, which help to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that can contribute to dandruff, reducing flaking and itching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Damask rose have anti-inflammatory effects, which can soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and inflammation associated with dandruff.

Moisturizing and emollient properties: The fatty acids and glycerides in Damask rose creams help to lock in moisture and nourish the scalp, providing relief from dryness and itchiness that can exacerbate dandruff.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosa centifolia, also known as damask rose, creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The rose's ability to soothe and calm the scalp reduces irritation and inflammation, which can contribute to dandruff. Additionally, the antifungal properties of damask rose help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common cause of dandruff. By using Rosa centifolia creams, individuals can experience relief from dandruff symptoms, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation associated with dandruff.
Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds can help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria that contribute to dandruff, thereby promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Salicylic acid: A beta-hydroxy acid, salicylic acid can help exfoliate the scalp, removing dead skin cells and other debris that can contribute to dandruff, while also reducing inflammation and itching.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Leaves: They contain compounds that exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to control dandruff-causing fungi and bacteria.
Seeds: They contain essential oils that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp associated with dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried rosa centifolia petals in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a 1:10 ratio for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Combine 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally until the wax melts.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken before filling into glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with dandruff because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's essential oils, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, penetrate deep into the scalp to combat fungal infections that can cause flaking and itching. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidants help soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp environment. By addressing the root causes of dandruff, rosemary creams provide long-lasting relief from this common scalp condition.
remedy_properties: Antifungal Properties: Rosemary's antifungal compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff by inhibiting the growth of yeast-like fungi.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties, including its ability to reduce prostaglandins, help soothe itchy scalps and reduce redness and inflammation associated with dandruff.

Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary's antioxidants, such as carnosic acid, help neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin and scalp, leading to premature aging and worsening of dandruff symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing scalp irritations and reducing inflammation associated with dandruff.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control fungal infections and reduce flaking caused by dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from your local market or apothecary.
Combine the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of pure coconut oil and heat mixture at 60 degrees celsius for two hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids, and let cool to room temperature.
Mix cooled oil with 50 grams of beeswax pellets and 20 milliliters of distilled water, then heat mixture at 70 degrees celsius until wax melts and mixture reaches a smooth consistency.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and label as herbal rosmarinus officinalis cream for dandruff relief, with preparation time approximately one hour total.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with dandruff because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as echinacea and salicylic acid, work together to soothe itchy scalps and reduce inflammation caused by dandruff. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of purple coneflower help to combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff. As a result, these creams provide natural relief from flaking, redness, and irritation associated with dandruff, promoting healthier and more balanced scalp conditions.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Purple coneflower cream's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of yeast and bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in purple coneflower cream soothe and calm itchy, flaky scalps, reducing redness and inflammation associated with dandruff.
Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's antioxidant properties help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dandruff symptoms and lead to further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective in soothing and treating itchy scalps.
Barks: The barks of the purple coneflower are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation, prevent fungal growth, and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and store them in an airtight container.
Combine the dried echinacea flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours at room temperature to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids after 15 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 20 milliliters of the infused echinacea oil and heat in a double boiler until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with dandruff because of its unique blend of natural ingredients. The creams contain extracts from the maidenhair tree, which is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp. This reduces redness and irritation, allowing the skin to breathe and function properly. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the scalp, reducing flaking and itchiness associated with dandruff, leaving the hair looking healthy and shiny.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal Maidenhair tree creams containing compounds like polyacetylenes and sesquiterpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and itching associated with dandruff.

Antifungal property: The cream's antifungal activity helps to combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff, such as Malassezia furfur, by inhibiting their growth and preventing them from producing irritating metabolites.

Soothing and moisturizing property: The creams' rich fatty acid content, particularly oleic and linoleic acids, helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with dandruff while also moisturizing the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, making it effective in soothing scalp irritations and fighting fungal infections causing dandruff.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide antioxidant properties to combat dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small saucepan for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil is fully infused with the herbal properties of ginkgo biloba.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container for 10 minutes, discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler until melted and well combined, then let cool to room temperature before whipping into a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which soothe and calm the scalp. The cream's ability to reduce redness and itching provides immediate relief from irritated skin, allowing flakes to naturally fall away. Additionally, chamomile's antibacterial qualities help prevent infection-causing fungi and bacteria from growing, reducing the risk of further irritation and flaking. As a result, regular use of chamomile creams can significantly reduce dandruff symptoms and promote a healthy, flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and bisabolol, help reduce redness and swelling associated with dandruff, allowing for a more comfortable scalp.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial properties of chamomile, including its ability to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, help combat the underlying fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff, reducing flaking and itching.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's natural sedative and anti-irritant compounds, such as matricine, calm irritated skin and reduce stress-related inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp environment and alleviating the discomfort associated with dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing redness and itching associated with dandruff.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes the scalp and reduces inflammation, helping to calm symptoms of dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground matricaria chamomilla powder with 1/2 cup of distilled water in a heatproof bowl, then steep it at room temperature for 2 hours.
Combine the cooled infusion with 8 ounces of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, stirring until melted.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler and let it cool to around 120°f, then whip it using an electric mixer for 5 minutes or until it thickens.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers, store them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 70°f, and use within 6 months or freeze for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredient, calendula, soothes itchy scalps and reduces redness, while also combating fungal infections that can cause flaking and irritation. Additionally, pot marigold's natural oils moisturize the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dandruff symptoms. As a result, using herbal pot marigold creams can provide effective relief from dandruff, leaving scalps feeling calm, comforted, and healthy-looking.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, itching, and scaling associated with dandruff, providing relief from irritated skin.
Antifungal property: The antifungal property of pot marigold cream inhibits the growth of Malassezia, a fungus that contributes to dandruff, thereby reducing flaking and irritation.
Emollient property: Pot marigold cream's emollient property helps to moisturize and soothe dry, flaky skin, allowing for better scaling removal and preventing further dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and itching caused by dandruff.
Seeds: Rich in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, soothing and calming irritated scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers that have been previously air-dried for 24 hours and store in an airtight container.
Grind 20g of the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground calendula with 10ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 2 drops of tea tree essential oil and stir well to combine, then remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
Pour the cooled mixture into a sterilized glass jar, label, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium creams helps with dandruff because of its antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream soothes an itchy scalp, reducing redness and inflammation caused by dandruff. It also prevents the growth of fungus that contributes to dandruff, helping to regulate the skin's natural pH balance. Additionally, wild geranium's antiseptic properties help to calm irritated skin, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing flaking. As a result, regular use of wild geranium creams can effectively alleviate symptoms of dandruff, leaving your scalp feeling calm, clear, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild geranium cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dandruff, providing relief to irritated scalps.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial agents in wild geranium cream combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing flaking.

Soothing and calming effects: Wild geranium's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds have a calming effect on the scalp, helping to reduce irritation and itching caused by dandruff, allowing for a more comfortable and peaceful scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antifungal and antibacterial compounds, making them effective against scalp infections that contribute to dandruff.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing flaking and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100 grams of geranium maculatum leaves and stems in a warm, well-ventilated area for two weeks.
Combine the dried plant material with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for four to six hours, allowing the herb to infuse its properties into the oil.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining 150 milliliters of infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler, heating until melted and blended, then pouring into sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with dandruff because of its unique ability to combat fungal infections, which are often a underlying cause of flaky, itchy scalp conditions. The herb's active compound, berberine, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of malassezia, a yeast that can contribute to dandruff. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the scalp and reduce redness and irritation, promoting a healthy and flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has anti-fungal, antibacterial, and antiviral properties that help combat fungal and yeast overgrowth on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff.

Isoquinoline Alkaloids: Isoquinoline alkaloids in goldenseal have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Berberastatin A: Berberastatin A has been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes involved in inflammation, which can contribute to dandruff symptoms such as flaking and scaling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing berberine, a natural antibacterial and antifungal agent, roots help combat fungal infections causing dandruff.
Rhizomes: Rich in berberine and other bioactive compounds, rhizomes have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe itchy scalps and reduce inflammation associated with dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried goldenseal root hydrastis canadensis and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered goldenseal with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler, melting the ingredients for 10 minutes.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency, then add 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of tea tree essential oil, stirring well for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 120°f within 30 minutes, allowing it to thicken slightly as it cools.
Pour the cooled mixture into a sterilized container, labeling it with the date prepared and ingredients used, and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with dandruff because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the scalp. The herbal extract contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and irritation, while also providing a cooling sensation that helps to alleviate itchiness. Additionally, stinging nettle has natural antifungal and antibacterial agents that work to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dandruff. As a result, the cream helps to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing flaking and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle creams can help reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff symptoms like redness, itchiness, and flaking. This anti-inflammatory effect helps calm down irritated skin and promotes a healthier scalp environment.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The nettle extracts in creams have been shown to exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities, which can help combat the underlying fungal or bacterial infections that can cause dandruff. By killing off these unwanted microorganisms, the cream can promote a balanced scalp ecosystem.

Soothing and calming effects: Nettle creams often contain compounds like flavonoids and lignans that have a soothing and calming impact on the skin, which can help reduce itching, irritation, and discomfort associated with dandruff. This comforting effect helps to calm down an itchy scalp and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, nettle roots help soothe itchy scalps and reduce inflammation associated with dandruff.
Buds: Nettle buds contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, combating fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves urtica dioica for infusion in boiling water.
Combine 20 grams of dried nettle leaves with 200 milliliters of distilled water for steeping.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes then strain and discard solids after 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of emulsifying wax with 50 grams of coconut oil and 25 grams of beeswax.
Add 20 milliliters of nettle infusion to the melted waxes then whip until thickened.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with dandruff because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that soothe and calm irritated scalps. The natural antioxidants and flavonoids present in dandelion extracts help to reduce redness, itching, and flaking, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of these creams hydrate the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness associated with dandruff, leaving hair looking shiny and healthy. By addressing the root causes of dandruff, dandelion creams provide long-lasting relief from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to scalp irritation, inflammation, and dandruff, promoting a healthier scalp environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory agents in dandelion cream reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dandruff, providing relief from discomfort and soothing the scalp.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Dandelion's antifungal and antibacterial compounds help to control fungal and bacterial overgrowth on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff, reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm itchy scalps.
Buds: High in triterpenoid saponins, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties that combat fungal infections causing dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh dandelion flowers and leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and contamination.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer on paper towels for 7-10 days, or until brittle.
Grind 20g of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 5g of the powdered taraxacum officinale with 30g of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any solids, then store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 weeks.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dandruff are natural topical treatments that help alleviate the symptoms of dandruff, a common scalp condition characterized by flakes of dead skin and irritation. These creams typically contain a combination of soothing and anti-inflammatory herbs that calm the scalp and reduce flaking. The best herbal creams for dandruff include Aloe vera, Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Azadirachta indica, Aloe barbadensis, Cymbopogon citratus, Curcuma longa, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Eucalyptus globulus, and Sesamum indicum. If left untreated, dandruff can lead to social anxiety, low self-esteem, and even affect one's professional life due to constant itching and embarrassment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dandruff that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and seborrheic dermatitis. These conditions cause inflammation and flaking of the skin, often leading to itching, redness, and scaling. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and burdock root can help soothe and calm these symptoms, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with dandruff is a blend of Tea Tree oil, Aloe Vera, and Coconut oil-based creams. Tea Tree oil's antifungal properties combat scalp infections, while Aloe Vera soothes and calms the scalp, reducing itchiness and flaking. Coconut oil's moisturizing properties help lock in hydration, preventing dryness and dandruff recurrence. This combination creates a balanced and nourishing formula that effectively treats dandruff, promoting a healthy and flake-free scalp. Regular application can lead to long-term relief and a manageable scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with dandruff because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The gel inside Aloe vera contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which work together to calm and hydrate the scalp. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of Aloe vera help to reduce the growth of yeast and bacteria that can contribute to dandruff, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and irritation, promoting a healthy scalp and relieving dandruff symptoms. Regular use can lead to a flake-free scalp.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Helps with dandruff by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which reduce redness and irritation on the scalp.
Aloin: Contributes to dandruff relief by preventing the growth of fungi and yeast that cause scalp infections, thereby promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin A and C, and Zinc): Aid in dandruff management by providing antioxidant properties, reducing inflammation, and promoting the health of scalp cells, ultimately leading to a reduction in flaking and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most used part due to their high concentration of aloin, a compound that provides anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties to combat dandruff.
Gel from Leaves: The gel inside the leaves has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dandruff symptoms.
Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: This extract is used due to its ability to reduce redness and irritation, providing relief from dandruff and promoting healthy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gently mix 1 cup of aloe vera gel with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a large mixing bowl.
Melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler for 5 minutes while continuously stirring.
Combine the melted beeswax with the aloe vera mixture and blend for 2 minutes using an electric mixer.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and refrigerate for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with dandruff because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which effectively combat the scalp infections and inflammation that lead to dandruff. The tea tree oil in these creams has been shown to reduce flaking and itching by regulating the scalp's natural pH and killing the Malassezia fungus, a common cause of dandruff. Additionally, Melaleuca alternifolia creams promote healthy scalp conditions by soothing and calming irritation, allowing for a balanced scalp and healthy hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These compounds help combat dandruff by reducing inflammation and preventing the buildup of dead skin cells on the scalp.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene possesses antimicrobial properties that help control fungal growth, contributing to the reduction of flaking and itching associated with dandruff.
Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which aid in soothing and calming the scalp, reducing irritation and the appearance of dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antifungal and antibacterial properties to soothe and treat dandruff.
Essential Oil (from Leaves): Used for its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties to treat and prevent dandruff.
Leaves' Essential Oil (Tea Tree Oil): Used for its antifungal, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties to combat dandruff-causing fungi and bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of melaleuca alternifolia oil and transfer it to a clean glass beaker.
Heat the oil in a double boiler to 40°c over a period of 30 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax in a separate glass container.
Slowly add the wax mixture to the heated melaleuca alternifolia oil and stir for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify over 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The herb, also known as rosemary, contains compounds like carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, which soothe the scalp and reduce irritation. By controlling fungal growth, rosemary cream prevents the buildup of dead skin cells that lead to dandruff. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to maintain a healthy scalp environment, promoting a balanced and flake-free scalp. Regular use of rosemary cream can lead to a reduction in dandruff symptoms and promote overall scalp health.
remedy_constituents: Carnacic acid: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, reducing the growth of fungi that can cause dandruff.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the scalp affected by dandruff.
Camphor: Possesses antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help in controlling the growth of dandruff-causing microorganisms and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary component used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly rosmarinic acid, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat dandruff.
Stems: Stems contain rosmarinic acid and other compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them useful in dandruff creams.
Flowers: Flowers are another key component used in dandruff creams due to their rosmarinic acid content, which helps soothe and calm the scalp while reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and smooth.
Add 2 tablespoons of rosmarinus officinalis essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well for 30 seconds.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 5 minutes or until it reaches 120°f to 140°f.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely to achieve a thick and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-fungal and antibacterial properties. The herbal ingredient, neem, found in these creams, inhibits the growth of fungi and bacteria that contribute to dandruff. The cream's soothing and calming effects help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dandruff, promoting healthy scalp conditions. Additionally, Azadirachta indica creams have been shown to balance the pH of the scalp, reducing flaking and promoting a smooth, healthy scalp. This makes it an effective natural remedy for dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Limonoids: These compounds have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control the growth of yeast and bacteria that contribute to dandruff.
Terpenoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with dandruff.

Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, thereby promoting healthy scalp and hair growth.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high concentration of neem oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help in treating dandruff.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a high amount of neem's bioactive compounds, which help in soothing the scalp and reducing flaking associated with dandruff.
Barks: The barks are used due to their rich content of triterpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that help in calming the scalp and reducing dandruff symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of azadirachta indica leaves and flowers on a dry morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind 100g of dried azadirachta indica leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix 20g of the azadirachta indica powder with 20g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes and stir occasionally to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to calm irritated scalps and reduce flaking. It also has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which prevent the growth of yeast and other microorganisms that contribute to dandruff. By keeping the scalp healthy and balanced, Aloe vera creams promote a smooth, flake-free hair and scalp. Regular use can lead to improved scalp health and a reduction in dandruff symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help with dandruff by creating a soothing and moisturizing effect on the scalp, reducing inflammation and flaking.
Aloin: Help with dandruff by inhibiting the growth of fungal and bacterial organisms that contribute to scalp irritation and flaking.
Acemannan: Help with dandruff by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, reducing redness and flaking associated with dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Gel extracted from the leaves is used due to its soothing and moisturizing properties, which help to calm and relieve dandruff symptoms.
Leaves: Extracts from the leaves also contain salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to reduce dandruff and promote healthy scalp skin.
Leaves: Aloe vera leaf juice contains vitamins and minerals that help to nourish and protect the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of aloe barbadensis gel to prepare the base for the cream.
Mix 20g of distilled water with 40g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Combine 10g of candelilla wax with 10g of coconut oil in the double boiler.
Stir in 10g of shea butter and 20g of aloe barbadensis gel into the mixture.
Filter the final mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The active compounds in the herb, such as limonene and geraniol, help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and redness. Additionally, the antifungal properties of Cymbopogon citratus creams help to control the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common contributor to dandruff. By reducing inflammation and controlling yeast growth, Cymbopogon citratus creams provide a natural and effective solution for dandruff sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Citronellal: Acts as an antifungal agent, helping to control the growth of fungi that can contribute to dandruff.
Geraniol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and irritation associated with dandruff.
Limonene: Possesses antiseptic properties, helping to eliminate bacteria and other pathogens that can exacerbate dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oils, particularly limonene and geraniol, exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat dandruff-causing fungi and bacteria.
Stems: The essential oils from the stems also contain limonene and geraniol, which aid in reducing fungal growth and inflammation associated with dandruff.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Cymbopogon citratus are rich in essential oils that contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dandruff symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract the essential oils from 20g of dried cymbopogon citratus by steam distillation for 2 hours.
Combine 100g of distilled water with 20g of glycerin and stir until the glycerin is fully dissolved.
Add 5g of beeswax to the water-glycerin mixture and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is fully melted.
Stir in 10g of the extracted cymbopogon citratus essential oil and 10g of vitamin e oil into the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with dandruff because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms the scalp. Turmeric, the active ingredient in Curcuma longa, has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that combat fungal infections and reduce inflammation, which are common causes of dandruff. By reducing redness and irritation, Curcuma longa creams promote a healthy scalp environment, allowing the skin to regenerate and reducing flaking, making it an effective and natural solution for managing dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps with dandruff by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and irritation associated with scalp conditions.
Tumerone: Contributes to dandruff relief by possessing antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of fungal species that may contribute to scalp infections.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Plays a role in dandruff treatment by displaying potent antioxidant properties, which can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm dandruff-prone scalps.
Roots: Roots are used for their rich content of essential oils, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control the growth of dandruff-causing yeast and bacteria.
Seeds: Seeds are used for their oil content, which has moisturizing and soothing properties that help to calm and hydrate the scalp, reducing dandruff and flaking.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50g of dried curcuma longa roots in 500ml of water for 2 hours at 95°c.
Filter the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate container to remove solid residue.
Combine the filtered liquid with 100g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes at 60°c while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum creams helps with dandruff because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon oil, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Malassezia yeast, a common trigger of dandruff. By reducing yeast overgrowth and inflammation, these creams create an ideal environment for healthy scalp hair growth. Regular application can lead to reduced flaking, itching, and redness, promoting a balanced and healthy scalp.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: It helps with dandruff by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and irritation of the scalp.
Eugenol: It helps with dandruff by possessing antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi that contribute to dandruff.
Cinnamyl acetate: It helps with dandruff by exhibiting antiseptic properties, reducing bacterial and fungal infections on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinnamomum zeylanicum leaves are used to make creams for dandruff due to their antifungal and antiseptic properties.
Barks: Cinnamomum zeylanicum barks are used to make creams for dandruff due to their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties.
Oils from leaves: Cinnamomum zeylanicum leaves oil is used to make creams for dandruff due to its antifungal properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1/2 cup of cinnamomum zeylanicum bark in 2 cups of coconut oil at 100°f for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whisk 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a separate bowl.
Gradually add the infused oil to the beeswax mixture while continuously whisking at 120°f for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredients, including eucalyptol and cineole, work to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking associated with dandruff. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Eucalyptus globulus help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff. Regular use of this cream can lead to a healthy and balanced scalp, reducing the appearance of dandruff and promoting a shiny, healthy-looking hair.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antifungal agent, reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of fungal species that contribute to dandruff.
Linalool: Possesses antiseptic and antifungal properties, helping to eliminate fungal infections and soothe irritated scalp, thereby reducing dandruff.
α-Terpineol: Exhibits antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment that prevents dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural antifungal and antibacterial agent.
Barks: Used for their rich content of tannins and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Essential Oil (derived from Leaves): Used due to its potent properties of reducing inflammation and fighting fungal infections that cause dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and 10 grams of vegetable oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and melt it in the double boiler for another 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum creams helps with dandruff because they contain sesamin, a natural compound that soothes and calms the scalp, reducing inflammation and itchiness associated with dandruff. The cream's antifungal properties also help control the growth of yeast-like fungus, Malassezia, which contributes to dandruff. Additionally, sesamum indicum has been traditionally used to promote a healthy scalp and reduce flaking, making it an effective natural remedy for managing dandruff symptoms and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_constituents: Sesamin: Sesamin helps with dandruff by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and itching associated with dandruff.
Sesquiterpenes: Sesquiterpenes help with dandruff by displaying antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi that contribute to dandruff.
Phenolic compounds: Phenolic compounds help with dandruff by exhibiting antioxidant properties, reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate dandruff symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high oil content, which is often extracted and incorporated into creams to help soothe and moisturize the scalp.
Leaves: Used due to their medicinal properties, including antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce dandruff and soothe irritated scalps.
Seeds (again): Used due to their high content of sesamin and sesamolin, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of sesamum indicum seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the sesamum indicum powder with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before whipping it with an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the sesamum indicum cream to a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
